Ambrosino also shares his vision for integrating tools like ChatGPT and Codex for coordinated workflows. This episode is worth listening to for those interested in AI-enhanced product work and software innovation.
summary.zh: 本期Lenny播客邀请了OpenAI Codex桌面应用开发负责人Andrew Ambrosino，讨论了AI如何改变产品开发、‘品味’作为AI优先工作环境中的职业技能的重要性、Codex应用的上线时机，以及OpenAI产品经理的‘区域防守’运作模式。Ambrosino还分享了整合ChatGPT和Codex实现协调工作流的愿景。对关注AI增强产品工作和软件创新者非常值得一听。
summary.es: Este episodio del Podcast de Lenny presenta a Andrew Ambrosino, líder del desarrollo de la aplicación de escritorio Codex en OpenAI. La discusión cubre cómo la IA ha transformado el desarrollo de productos, la importancia del 'gusto' como habilidad profesional en un entorno de trabajo centrado en IA, detalles prácticos sobre el lanzamiento de la app Codex y cómo los gerentes de producto en OpenAI operan bajo un modelo de 'defensa por zonas'. Ambrosino también comparte su visión para integrar herramientas como ChatGPT y Codex en flujos de trabajo coordinados. Este episodio es valioso para interesados en el trabajo de producto potenciado por IA y la innovación en software.

title.original: The next big breakthrough will be AIs learning on the job
title.en: The next big breakthrough will be AIs learning on the job
title.zh: 下一大突破将是AI的在职学习
title.es: El próximo gran avance será que las IAs aprendan en el trabajo

summary.original: In the Dwarkesh Podcast episode titled 'The next big breakthrough will be AIs learning on the job,' the discussion centers on the current approach in AI research that focuses on training models across numerous tasks and reinforcement learning environments to approach general intelligence. The episode highlights challenges like sample inefficiency and the difficulty of continual learning with real-world data. It argues that AIs will need much longer context windows and better learning from sparse, ambiguous interactions to acquire complex human skills. The episode may appeal to listeners interested in AI training paradigms and the limits of current AI learning methods.
summary.en: In the Dwarkesh Podcast episode titled 'The next big breakthrough will be AIs learning on the job,' the discussion centers on the current approach in AI research that focuses on training models across numerous tasks and reinforcement learning environments to approach general intelligence. The episode highlights challenges like sample inefficiency and the difficulty of continual learning with real-world data. It argues that AIs will need much longer context windows and better learning from sparse, ambiguous interactions to acquire complex human skills. The episode may appeal to listeners interested in AI training paradigms and the limits of current AI learning methods.
summary.zh: Dwarkesh播客探讨了当前AI研究中通过多任务训练和强化学习环境追求通用智能的方式，指出采样效率低和难以持续从真实数据中学习的挑战。论述了AI需更长的上下文窗口及更好地从稀疏且模糊的交互中学习，以掌握复杂人类技能。适合关注AI训练范式及当前学习方法局限的听众。
summary.es: En el episodio del Podcast de Dwarkesh titulado 'El próximo gran avance será que las IAs aprendan en el trabajo', se analiza el enfoque actual en la investigación de IA que entrena modelos en múltiples tareas y entornos de aprendizaje por refuerzo para acercarse a la inteligencia general. Se destacan desafíos como la ineficiencia muestral y la dificultad del aprendizaje continuo con datos reales. Se argumenta que las IAs necesitarán ventanas de contexto más largas y mejor aprendizaje a partir de interacciones escasas y ambiguas para adquirir habilidades humanas complejas. Este episodio puede interesar a quienes estudian los paradigmas de entrenamiento de IA y los límites de los métodos actuales.

title.original: Really Big Test-Time Compute in AI Changes Benchmarks, Safety and Research with OpenAI Research Scientist Noam Brown
title.en: Really Big Test-Time Compute in AI Changes Benchmarks, Safety and Research with OpenAI Research Scientist Noam Brown
title.zh: AI中超大测试计算改变基准、安全与研究——OpenAI科学家Noam Brown访谈
title.es: Cálculo masivo en tiempo de prueba en IA cambia benchmarks, seguridad e investigación con el científico de OpenAI Noam Brown

summary.original: This No Priors Podcast episode features OpenAI research scientist Noam Brown discussing issues with traditional AI benchmarks that use fixed evaluation grids not accounting for model thinking time. The conversation covers how incorporating large-scale test-time compute changes how AI models are evaluated, affecting assessments of capability, safety, and research directions. This episode offers insights into evolving AI evaluation methods and is relevant for listeners interested in AI model performance and benchmarking.
summary.en: This No Priors Podcast episode features OpenAI research scientist Noam Brown discussing issues with traditional AI benchmarks that use fixed evaluation grids not accounting for model thinking time. The conversation covers how incorporating large-scale test-time compute changes how AI models are evaluated, affecting assessments of capability, safety, and research directions. This episode offers insights into evolving AI evaluation methods and is relevant for listeners interested in AI model performance and benchmarking.
summary.zh: No Priors播客邀请OpenAI研究员Noam Brown，讨论传统AI基准测试使用固定评估网格未考虑模型思考时间的问题。谈及大规模测试时计算如何影响AI模型评估，进而影响能力、安全和研究方向评估。本期为关注AI性能和基准测试方法演变的听众提供深刻见解。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast No Priors presenta al científico investigador de OpenAI Noam Brown discutiendo problemas con benchmarks tradicionales de IA que usan rejillas fijas sin considerar el tiempo de pensamiento del modelo. La conversación aborda cómo la incorporación de gran cálculo en tiempo de prueba cambia la evaluación de modelos de IA, afectando evaluaciones de capacidad, seguridad y direcciones de investigación. Ofrece perspectivas sobre la evolución de métodos de evaluación de IA, relevante para quienes siguen desempeño y benchmarking de modelos.

title.original: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.en: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.zh: AI核心的数据信息黑洞
title.es: El agujero negro de datos en el centro de la IA

summary.original: The Dwarkesh YouTube video 'The data black hole at the center of AI' emphasizes the vast amount of data used to train AI models, far surpassing what humans experience in a lifetime. It reflects on how AI is often viewed as a galaxy of information, underscoring the scale and complexity of AI training data. This video could interest those curious about the data foundations of AI and its scale, though the evidence does not provide more detail about specific topics discussed.
summary.en: The Dwarkesh YouTube video 'The data black hole at the center of AI' emphasizes the vast amount of data used to train AI models, far surpassing what humans experience in a lifetime. It reflects on how AI is often viewed as a galaxy of information, underscoring the scale and complexity of AI training data. This video could interest those curious about the data foundations of AI and its scale, though the evidence does not provide more detail about specific topics discussed.
summary.zh: Dwarkesh的YouTube视频强调训练AI模型所用数据量巨大，远超人类一生所经历的信息量，形象比喻AI为信息星系，凸显AI训练数据的规模与复杂性。适合对AI数据基础及其规模感兴趣的观众。
summary.es: El video de YouTube de Dwarkesh 'El agujero negro de datos en el centro de la IA' enfatiza la enorme cantidad de datos utilizados para entrenar modelos de IA, que supera con creces la experiencia vital de un humano. Reflexiona sobre cómo la IA se ve como una galaxia de información, subrayando la escala y complejidad de los datos de entrenamiento. Puede interesar a quienes quieran conocer las bases de datos de la IA y su escala, aunque no se proporcionan detalles específicos.

title.original: What happens after coding is solved? | Fiona Fung (Claude Code & Cowork)
title.en: What happens after coding is solved? | Fiona Fung (Claude Code & Cowork)
title.zh: 编码难题解决后会发生什么？| Fiona Fung（Claude Code & Cowork）
title.es: ¿Qué pasa después de que se resuelve la codificación? | Fiona Fung (Claude Code & Cowork)

summary.original: This episode of Lenny's Podcast features Fiona Fung, who leads the teams behind Claude Code and Cowork at Anthropic, including overseeing Boris Cherny and the engineering and PM teams. The discussion explores what happens after coding is solved, touching on topics related to product, AI, technology, and developer perspectives. This episode may be worth listening to for those interested in insights from a leader managing AI product teams at Anthropic.
summary.en: This episode of Lenny's Podcast features Fiona Fung, who leads the teams behind Claude Code and Cowork at Anthropic, including overseeing Boris Cherny and the engineering and PM teams. The discussion explores what happens after coding is solved, touching on topics related to product, AI, technology, and developer perspectives. This episode may be worth listening to for those interested in insights from a leader managing AI product teams at Anthropic.
summary.zh: 本期Lenny播客邀请Anthropic的Fiona Fung，负责Claude Code和Cowork团队，包括Boris Cherny及其工程和产品管理团队。讨论编码难题解决后涉及的产品、AI、技术及开发者视角。适合关注Anthropic AI产品团队领导见解的听众。
summary.es: Este episodio del Podcast de Lenny cuenta con Fiona Fung, quien lidera los equipos detrás de Claude Code y Cowork en Anthropic, supervisando a Boris Cherny y los equipos de ingeniería y gestión de producto. La charla explora qué ocurre una vez que la codificación está resuelta, abordando temas relacionados con producto, IA, tecnología y perspectivas de desarrolladores. Recomendado para quienes buscan perspectivas de líderes en equipos de producto de IA en Anthropic.

title.original: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
title.en: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
title.zh: 打造一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
title.es: Construyendo una escuela donde los modelos de IA aprenden sobre la humanidad

summary.original: The AI & I podcast episode titled "Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity" discusses themes around AI scaling laws and a future where AI may outperform humans in many tasks. The episode features Edwin Chen, CEO of Surge AI, who shares his perspective on AI development trajectories and the relationship between AI models and human skills. This episode is relevant for listeners interested in AI's evolving capabilities and educational approaches for AI systems.
summary.en: The AI & I podcast episode titled "Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity" discusses themes around AI scaling laws and a future where AI may outperform humans in many tasks. The episode features Edwin Chen, CEO of Surge AI, who shares his perspective on AI development trajectories and the relationship between AI models and human skills. This episode is relevant for listeners interested in AI's evolving capabilities and educational approaches for AI systems.
summary.zh: AI & I播客探讨AI扩展规律及未来AI超过人类完成多项任务的可能性。节目嘉宾Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen分享对AI发展轨迹及其与人类技能关系的看法。适合关注AI能力演化和AI教育方法的听众。
summary.es: El episodio del podcast AI & I titulado 'Construyendo una escuela donde los modelos de IA aprenden sobre la humanidad' aborda temas sobre las leyes de escalabilidad de IA y un futuro donde la IA pueda superar a los humanos en muchas tareas. Presenta a Edwin Chen, CEO de Surge AI, quien comparte su perspectiva sobre las trayectorias del desarrollo de IA y la relación entre los modelos de IA y las habilidades humanas. Relevante para interesados en la evolución de capacidades de IA y enfoques educativos para sistemas de IA.

title.original: Memory and Continual Learning: Engram's Dan Biderman and Jessy Lin
title.en: Memory and Continual Learning: Engram's Dan Biderman and Jessy Lin
title.zh: 记忆与持续学习：Engram的Dan Biderman与Jessy Lin
title.es: Memoria y aprendizaje continuo: Dan Biderman y Jessy Lin de Engram

summary.original: In this Training Data podcast episode, Dan Biderman and Jessy Lin, co-founders of Engram, discuss their work focusing on memory and continual learning, which they consider to be two sides of the same coin. The episode centers on how these concepts impact AI and technology development. Listeners interested in AI advancements in learning and memory mechanisms may find this episode insightful.
summary.en: In this Training Data podcast episode, Dan Biderman and Jessy Lin, co-founders of Engram, discuss their work focusing on memory and continual learning, which they consider to be two sides of the same coin. The episode centers on how these concepts impact AI and technology development. Listeners interested in AI advancements in learning and memory mechanisms may find this episode insightful.
summary.zh: Training Data播客邀请Engram联合创始人Dan Biderman与Jessy Lin，讨论记忆与持续学习作为一个整体的概念及其对AI和技术发展的影响。对关注AI学习与记忆机制进展的听众颇具启发意义。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast Training Data, Dan Biderman y Jessy Lin, cofundadores de Engram, discuten su trabajo centrado en la memoria y el aprendizaje continuo, considerándolos dos caras de la misma moneda. El episodio se enfoca en cómo estos conceptos impactan el desarrollo de la IA y la tecnología. Puede ser útil para quienes estén interesados en avances en mecanismos de aprendizaje y memoria de IA.

title.original: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
title.en: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
title.zh: 前沿生态必须开放——Databricks联合创始人Matei Zaharia与Reynold Xin
title.es: Por qué el ecosistema Frontier debe ser abierto — Matei Zaharia y Reynold Xin, Databricks

summary.original: The Latent Space Podcast features a rare double-interview with Databricks co-founders Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin discussing the evolution of Databricks from big data company to an operating system for enterprise AI agents. They cover open-source projects like Omnigent, addressing challenges around AI agent collaboration, security, and integration across platforms. Technical topics include Databricks' database innovations such as LTAP and Lakebase architecture, and the importance of delivering the right data context to AI at the right moment. The episode offers a comprehensive look into how Databricks is shaping agent ecosystems, making it valuable for listeners interested in AI infrastructure and enterprise AI innovation.
summary.en: The Latent Space Podcast features a rare double-interview with Databricks co-founders Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin discussing the evolution of Databricks from big data company to an operating system for enterprise AI agents. They cover open-source projects like Omnigent, addressing challenges around AI agent collaboration, security, and integration across platforms. Technical topics include Databricks' database innovations such as LTAP and Lakebase architecture, and the importance of delivering the right data context to AI at the right moment. The episode offers a comprehensive look into how Databricks is shaping agent ecosystems, making it valuable for listeners interested in AI infrastructure and enterprise AI innovation.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客罕见地采访Databricks联合创始人Matei Zaharia和Reynold Xin，谈论Databricks从大数据公司转型为企业AI代理操作系统。内容涵盖开源项目Omnigent，AI代理协作、安全与平台整合挑战，以及LTAP和Lakebase架构等数据库创新。强调提供实时恰当数据上下文的重要性。本期为关注AI基础设施与企业AI创新者提供全面视角。
summary.es: El podcast Latent Space presenta una doble entrevista con los cofundadores de Databricks, Matei Zaharia y Reynold Xin, discutiendo la evolución de Databricks de empresa de big data a sistema operativo para agentes IA empresariales. Cubren proyectos open source como Omnigent, desafíos en colaboración, seguridad e integración de agentes IA en plataformas, innovaciones en base de datos como LTAP y arquitectura Lakebase, y la importancia de entregar el contexto adecuado a la IA en el momento justo. Ofrece una visión completa para interesados en infraestructura y innovación IA empresarial.

title.original: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
title.en: Red-Teaming after Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
title.zh: Mythos之后的红队测试 — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan
title.es: Red-Teaming después de Mythos — Zico Kolter & Matt Fredrikson, Gray Swan

summary.original: This Latent Space podcast episode features Zico Kolter, an OpenAI board member, and Matt Fredrikson, CEO of Gray Swan, discussing AI security. They explain why AI security differs from traditional cybersecurity, focusing on vulnerabilities like prompt injection and the emerging field of automated red teaming. The episode delves into AI robustness, agent vulnerabilities, and the potential future of AI security including AI-driven attack and defense mechanisms. It's valuable for listeners interested in advanced AI safety and security topics.
summary.en: This Latent Space podcast episode features Zico Kolter, an OpenAI board member, and Matt Fredrikson, CEO of Gray Swan, discussing AI security. They explain why AI security differs from traditional cybersecurity, focusing on vulnerabilities like prompt injection and the emerging field of automated red teaming. The episode delves into AI robustness, agent vulnerabilities, and the potential future of AI security including AI-driven attack and defense mechanisms. It's valuable for listeners interested in advanced AI safety and security topics.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客中，OpenAI董事Zico Kolter与Gray Swan CEO Matt Fredrikson讨论AI安全，解释其与传统网络安全不同，聚焦提示注入、自动红队测试、AI鲁棒性及未来AI安全发展。内容适合关注先进AI安全与防护的听众。
summary.es: En este episodio de Latent Space, Zico Kolter (miembro del consejo de OpenAI) y Matt Fredrikson (CEO de Gray Swan) discuten la seguridad en IA, diferenciándola de la ciberseguridad tradicional, enfocándose en vulnerabilidades como la inyección de prompts y el red-teaming automatizado, explorando la robustez de IA y el futuro de la seguridad IA, valioso para interesados en seguridad avanzada de IA.

title.original: #397 ‒ Endometriosis and adenomyosis: diagnosis, fertility, reproductive aging, and emerging treatments | Renato Tomioka, M.D., Ph.D.
title.en: #397 ‒ Endometriosis and adenomyosis: diagnosis, fertility, reproductive aging, and emerging treatments | Renato Tomioka, M.D., Ph.D.
title.zh: #397 ‒ 子宫内膜异位症与子宫腺肌症：诊断、生育力、女性生育衰老及新兴治疗 | Renato Tomioka博士
title.es: #397 ‒ Endometriosis y adenomiosis: diagnóstico, fertilidad, envejecimiento reproductivo y tratamientos emergentes | Renato Tomioka, M.D., Ph.D.

summary.original: Episode #397 of The Peter Attia Drive features Dr. Renato Tomioka, a specialist in reproductive medicine and gynecologic surgery, discussing the diagnosis and treatment of endometriosis and adenomyosis. The episode covers how these conditions are often undiagnosed despite affecting millions of women worldwide, advancements in diagnostic imaging, decision-making between hormonal therapy and surgery, fertility preservation strategies, the impact of female age on fertility, common mistakes in treatment, and emerging therapies. Dr. Tomioka highlights the benefits of earlier diagnosis in reducing disease burden, offering a detailed and evidence-informed exploration of women's reproductive health.
summary.en: Episode #397 of The Peter Attia Drive features Dr. Renato Tomioka, a specialist in reproductive medicine and gynecologic surgery, discussing the diagnosis and treatment of endometriosis and adenomyosis. The episode covers how these conditions are often undiagnosed despite affecting millions of women worldwide, advancements in diagnostic imaging, decision-making between hormonal therapy and surgery, fertility preservation strategies, the impact of female age on fertility, common mistakes in treatment, and emerging therapies. Dr. Tomioka highlights the benefits of earlier diagnosis in reducing disease burden, offering a detailed and evidence-informed exploration of women's reproductive health.
summary.zh: 《The Peter Attia Drive》第397集，生殖医学及妇科手术专家Renato Tomioka博士讨论子宫内膜异位症与子宫腺肌症的诊断与治疗，涵盖诊断成像进展、生育力保护、年龄影响、常见治疗误区及新疗法，强调早期诊断降低疾病负担，对女性生殖健康有深入且有证据支持的讲解。
summary.es: En el episodio #397 de The Peter Attia Drive, el Dr. Renato Tomioka, especialista en medicina reproductiva y cirugía ginecológica, aborda el diagnóstico y tratamiento de la endometriosis y adenomiosis, incluyendo avances en imagenología, decisiones entre terapias hormonales y cirugía, estrategias de preservación de fertilidad, impacto de la edad, errores comunes y terapias emergentes, destacando los beneficios del diagnóstico temprano para reducir la carga de la enfermedad, ofreciendo una exploración detallada y basada en evidencia sobre salud reproductiva femenina.

title.original: What happens after coding is solved? | Fiona Fung (Manager of the Claude Code and Cowork Teams)
title.en: What happens after coding is solved? | Fiona Fung (Manager of the Claude Code and Cowork Teams)
title.zh: 解决编码问题后会发生什么？| Fiona Fung（Claude Code和Cowork团队经理）
title.es: ¿Qué sucede después de resolver la codificación? | Fiona Fung (Gerente de los equipos Claude Code y Cowork)

summary.original: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Fiona Fung discusses her leadership of the Claude Code and Cowork teams at Anthropic, overseeing engineering and product teams responsible for shipping significantly more code than before. She shares insight into how software development roles are evolving with AI, how her team integrates AI into their workflows, and challenges like context-switching. Fiona's extensive engineering career at Microsoft and Meta provides a unique perspective on the transformation of building software and managing teams in an AI-driven environment.
summary.en: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Fiona Fung discusses her leadership of the Claude Code and Cowork teams at Anthropic, overseeing engineering and product teams responsible for shipping significantly more code than before. She shares insight into how software development roles are evolving with AI, how her team integrates AI into their workflows, and challenges like context-switching. Fiona's extensive engineering career at Microsoft and Meta provides a unique perspective on the transformation of building software and managing teams in an AI-driven environment.
summary.zh: 在Lenny的播客中，Fiona Fung介绍她领导Anthropic Claude Code和Cowork团队的经验，分享AI如何改变软件开发角色及团队工作流程，讨论团队如何整合AI及上下文切换的挑战，结合她在微软和Meta的丰富工程经验，提供AI驱动环境下构建软件和管理团队的独特见解。
summary.es: En este episodio del Podcast de Lenny, Fiona Fung habla sobre su liderazgo de los equipos Claude Code y Cowork en Anthropic, explicando cómo los roles en desarrollo de software están evolucionando con la IA, cómo integran la IA en sus flujos de trabajo y los desafíos como el cambio de contexto. Su amplia experiencia en ingeniería en Microsoft y Meta ofrece una perspectiva única sobre la transformación de construir software y gestionar equipos en un entorno impulsado por IA.

title.original: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.en: The data black hole at the center of AI
title.zh: 人工智能核心的数据黑洞
title.es: El agujero negro de datos en el centro de la IA

summary.original: Dwarkesh Patel's podcast episode titled "The data black hole at the center of AI" examines the concept of sample efficiency in AI and the immense volumes of expert-labeled data required to train modern AI models. The episode explains how current AI progress is driven more by data quality and quantity than by architectural innovations, describing an analogy of AI as a galaxy held together by a massive black hole of data. It also compares human learning efficiency to that of AI, highlighting the vast differences in data requirements. This detailed exploration offers valuable insight into the challenges of AI training and data dependence.
summary.en: Dwarkesh Patel's podcast episode titled "The data black hole at the center of AI" examines the concept of sample efficiency in AI and the immense volumes of expert-labeled data required to train modern AI models. The episode explains how current AI progress is driven more by data quality and quantity than by architectural innovations, describing an analogy of AI as a galaxy held together by a massive black hole of data. It also compares human learning efficiency to that of AI, highlighting the vast differences in data requirements. This detailed exploration offers valuable insight into the challenges of AI training and data dependence.
summary.zh: Dwarkesh Patel的播客《人工智能核心的数据黑洞》探讨了样本效率及训练现代AI模型所需的大量专家标注数据，指出当前AI进展更多依赖数据质量和数量而非架构创新，将AI比作被数据黑洞维系的星系，并比较了人类与AI的学习效率差异，深入解析了AI训练和数据依赖的挑战。
summary.es: El episodio del podcast de Dwarkesh Patel titulado "El agujero negro de datos en el centro de la IA" examina la eficiencia de muestras en IA y el gran volumen de datos etiquetados por expertos necesarios para entrenar modelos modernos, explicando cómo el progreso actual de la IA depende más de la calidad y cantidad de datos que de innovaciones arquitectónicas, comparando el aprendizaje humano con el de la IA y ofreciendo una visión detallada de los desafíos en el entrenamiento y dependencia de datos.

title.original: The Professor of Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP
title.en: The Professor of Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP
title.zh: 输出最大化教授——Anjney Midha，AMP
title.es: El Profesor de Outputmaxxing — Anjney Midha, AMP

summary.original: This Latent Space Podcast episode features Anjney Midha, founder of AMP, exploring the complexities of AI compute efficiency and the scaling challenges in frontier AI labs. He discusses why simply acquiring more GPUs doesn't guarantee better models and the importance of system-level improvements such as scheduling, networking, and utilization. Anjney shares insights into AMP's vision for a compute grid that optimizes FLOP usage, the limitations of current AI infrastructure, and the cultural and leadership aspects influencing AI research organizations. His background spans work at major tech companies and investing in AI startups.
summary.en: This Latent Space Podcast episode features Anjney Midha, founder of AMP, exploring the complexities of AI compute efficiency and the scaling challenges in frontier AI labs. He discusses why simply acquiring more GPUs doesn't guarantee better models and the importance of system-level improvements such as scheduling, networking, and utilization. Anjney shares insights into AMP's vision for a compute grid that optimizes FLOP usage, the limitations of current AI infrastructure, and the cultural and leadership aspects influencing AI research organizations. His background spans work at major tech companies and investing in AI startups.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客邀请AMP创始人Anjney Midha，探讨AI算力效率复杂性及前沿AI实验室的扩展挑战。他讨论为何单纯增加GPU数量不保证模型提升，强调系统级改进如调度、网络和利用率的重要性。Anjney介绍AMP关于算力网格优化FLOP使用的愿景，当前AI基础设施限制，以及影响AI研究机构的文化与领导力。他曾在顶级科技公司工作并投资AI初创企业。
summary.es: En este episodio del Latent Space Podcast, Anjney Midha, fundador de AMP, explora las complejidades de la eficiencia del cómputo en IA y los desafíos de escalabilidad en laboratorios de IA de frontera. Discute por qué simplemente adquirir más GPUs no garantiza mejores modelos y la importancia de mejoras a nivel de sistema como programación, redes y utilización. Anjney comparte la visión de AMP para una red de cómputo que optimice el uso de FLOPs, las limitaciones de la infraestructura actual y los aspectos culturales y de liderazgo que influyen en las organizaciones de investigación en IA. Su experiencia incluye trabajo en grandes empresas tecnológicas e inversión en startups de IA.

title.original: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
title.en: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
title.zh: 马基雅维利：被误解最深的思想家 —— 阿达·帕尔默
title.es: Maquiavelo: el pensador más incomprendido de todos los tiempos – Ada Palmer

summary.original: This episode features Ada Palmer discussing Machiavelli, whom she describes as perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time. The conversation likely explores Machiavelli's historical role and intellectual contributions, based on his background as a high-level diplomat. It may provide listeners with fresh perspectives on Machiavelli's work and influence.
summary.en: This episode features Ada Palmer discussing Machiavelli, whom she describes as perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time. The conversation likely explores Machiavelli's historical role and intellectual contributions, based on his background as a high-level diplomat. It may provide listeners with fresh perspectives on Machiavelli's work and influence.
summary.zh: 这一集由阿达·帕尔默讲述，她认为马基雅维利可能是历史上被误解最深的思想家，探讨了他的历史角色和智识贡献，尤其是作为一名高级外交官的背景，为听众提供了关于马基雅维利作品和影响的新视角。
summary.es: Este episodio presenta a Ada Palmer discutiendo a Maquiavelo, a quien describe como quizás el pensador más incomprendido de todos los tiempos, explorando su papel histórico y contribuciones intelectuales basado en su experiencia como diplomático de alto nivel, ofreciendo nuevas perspectivas sobre su obra e influencia.

title.original: GitHub’s COO Explains Why AI Hasn’t Replaced Developers
title.en: GitHub’s COO Explains Why AI Hasn’t Replaced Developers
title.zh: GitHub首席运营官解读为何AI尚未取代开发者
title.es: COO de GitHub explica por qué la IA no ha reemplazado a los desarrolladores

summary.original: In this podcast episode, GitHub's COO Kyle Daigle discusses why artificial intelligence has not replaced software developers. The episode likely touches on trends such as the dramatic increase in GitHub commits from 1 billion to an expected 14 billion, suggesting an expansion in development activity rather than reduction. This conversation could offer insights into AI's role in software development.
summary.en: In this podcast episode, GitHub's COO Kyle Daigle discusses why artificial intelligence has not replaced software developers. The episode likely touches on trends such as the dramatic increase in GitHub commits from 1 billion to an expected 14 billion, suggesting an expansion in development activity rather than reduction. This conversation could offer insights into AI's role in software development.
summary.zh: GitHub首席运营官Kyle Daigle在播客中讨论了人工智能尚未取代软件开发者的原因。节目提及GitHub提交量从10亿激增至预计140亿，显示开发活动在扩展而非减少，提供了AI在软件开发中角色的见解。
summary.es: En este episodio, el COO de GitHub, Kyle Daigle, discute por qué la inteligencia artificial no ha reemplazado a los desarrolladores de software, tocando temas como el aumento dramático de commits en GitHub de 1 mil millones a 14 mil millones estimados, sugiriendo una expansión de la actividad de desarrollo y ofreciendo perspectivas sobre el rol de la IA en el desarrollo de software.

title.original: Re-engineering the Semiconductor Supply Chain with Intel CEO Lip Bu Tan
title.en: Re-engineering the Semiconductor Supply Chain with Intel CEO Lip Bu Tan
title.zh: 英特尔CEO谭立波谈半导体供应链再造
title.es: Reingeniería de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores con el CEO de Intel, Lip Bu Tan

summary.original: The episode features Intel CEO Lip Bu Tan discussing efforts to re-engineer the semiconductor supply chain. Noted as a former Cadence CEO and investor, Lip Bu Tan, at age 66, has undertaken this challenging role in the tech industry. Listeners interested in semiconductor industry challenges and strategies might find this conversation valuable.
summary.en: The episode features Intel CEO Lip Bu Tan discussing efforts to re-engineer the semiconductor supply chain. Noted as a former Cadence CEO and investor, Lip Bu Tan, at age 66, has undertaken this challenging role in the tech industry. Listeners interested in semiconductor industry challenges and strategies might find this conversation valuable.
summary.zh: 本期节目由英特尔CEO谭立波分享他在重塑半导体供应链方面的努力。谭立波曾任Cadence CEO和投资人，现年66岁，承担科技行业的这一挑战，适合关注半导体行业挑战与策略的听众。
summary.es: El episodio presenta al CEO de Intel, Lip Bu Tan, hablando sobre sus esfuerzos para reingeniería la cadena de suministro de semiconductores. Ex CEO de Cadence e inversor, Lip Bu Tan, a sus 66 años, afronta este desafío en la industria tecnológica, ofreciendo una valiosa conversación para interesados en desafíos y estrategias del sector.

title.original: Re-engineering the Semiconductor Supply Chain with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan
title.en: Re-engineering the Semiconductor Supply Chain with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan
title.zh: 与Intel CEO谭立布共话半导体供应链再造
title.es: Reingeniería de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores con el CEO de Intel, Lip-Bu Tan

summary.original: The No Priors Podcast features a conversation with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, focusing on his efforts to lead Intel's turnaround. The discussion covers how his experience in startup culture influences his approach to driving changes in Intel's corporate culture and operational strategies. The episode explores the challenges and strategies involved in re-engineering the semiconductor supply chain within a leading technology company.
summary.en: The No Priors Podcast features a conversation with Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan, focusing on his efforts to lead Intel's turnaround. The discussion covers how his experience in startup culture influences his approach to driving changes in Intel's corporate culture and operational strategies. The episode explores the challenges and strategies involved in re-engineering the semiconductor supply chain within a leading technology company.
summary.zh: No Priors播客专访Intel CEO谭立布，聚焦其领导Intel转型的努力。讨论涵盖其创业文化经验如何影响推动Intel公司文化和运营策略变革。节目探讨在领先科技公司内重塑半导体供应链面临的挑战与策略。
summary.es: El podcast No Priors presenta una conversación con el CEO de Intel, Lip-Bu Tan, enfocándose en sus esfuerzos para liderar la transformación de Intel. La discusión cubre cómo su experiencia en cultura startup influye en su enfoque para impulsar cambios en la cultura corporativa y estrategias operativas de Intel. El episodio explora los desafíos y estrategias involucrados en reingeniería de la cadena de suministro de semiconductores dentro de una compañía tecnológica líder.

title.original: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
title.en: 🔬 The Self-Driving Lab — Joseph Krause, Radical AI
title.zh: 自动化实验室驱动创新——Joseph Krause，Radical AI
title.es: 🔬 El laboratorio autónomo — Joseph Krause, Radical AI

summary.original: On this episode of the Latent Space Podcast, Joseph Krause, founder of Radical AI, discusses accelerating materials science innovation through self-driving labs. He explains how Radical AI's automated labs, augmented by an "AI scientist," can generate and test hypotheses rapidly, speeding the materials discovery process by nearly tenfold compared to previous programs. The episode delves into challenges unique to materials science like complex manufacturing processes and supply chains, emphasizing that experimental data and lab automation form the industry's competitive moat. Joseph also addresses the role of public-private partnerships in advancing these technologies and shares Radical AI's commitment to open-sourcing key tools.
summary.en: On this episode of the Latent Space Podcast, Joseph Krause, founder of Radical AI, discusses accelerating materials science innovation through self-driving labs. He explains how Radical AI's automated labs, augmented by an "AI scientist," can generate and test hypotheses rapidly, speeding the materials discovery process by nearly tenfold compared to previous programs. The episode delves into challenges unique to materials science like complex manufacturing processes and supply chains, emphasizing that experimental data and lab automation form the industry's competitive moat. Joseph also addresses the role of public-private partnerships in advancing these technologies and shares Radical AI's commitment to open-sourcing key tools.
summary.zh: Latent Space播客中，Radical AI创始人Joseph Krause谈加速材料科学创新的自动化实验室。他解释了由“AI科学家”辅助的自动化实验室如何快速生成并测试假设，使材料发现速度相比以往提升近十倍。节目探讨材料科学中复杂制造与供应链的挑战，强调实验数据和实验室自动化构成行业竞争壁垒。Joseph还谈及公私合作推动技术发展及Radical AI开源关键工具的承诺。
summary.es: En este episodio del Latent Space Podcast, Joseph Krause, fundador de Radical AI, discute la aceleración de la innovación en ciencia de materiales mediante laboratorios autónomos. Explica cómo los laboratorios automatizados de Radical AI, aumentados por un "científico de IA", pueden generar y probar hipótesis rápidamente, acelerando el proceso de descubrimiento de materiales casi diez veces comparado con programas anteriores. El episodio aborda desafíos únicos de la ciencia de materiales como procesos de manufactura complejos y cadenas de suministro, enfatizando que los datos experimentales y la automatización de laboratorios forman la ventaja competitiva de la industria. Joseph también comenta el papel de colaboraciones público-privadas y el compromiso de Radical AI con el código abierto.

title.original: Ada Palmer – Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time
title.en: Ada Palmer – Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time
title.zh: Ada Palmer —— 马基雅维利是史上被误解最深的思想家
title.es: Ada Palmer – Machiavelli es el pensador más malinterpretado de todos los tiempos

summary.original: This episode of Dwarkesh Podcast features Ada Palmer discussing Niccolò Machiavelli, highlighting how he is perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time. The conversation covers Machiavelli's career as a high-level diplomat for Florence, his observations of European rulers, and his political downfall when the Medici retook Florence in 1513, leading to his torture and exile. Despite this, Machiavelli dedicated his seminal book, The Prince, to Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici, the leader of the regime responsible for his exile, aiming to use his insights to protect Florence. The episode explores Machiavelli's lessons on power, politics, and human nature and how his experiences shaped his work. Ada Palmer is also launching a new podcast focusing on Machiavelli, making this episode useful for those interested in deeper understanding of his work and historical context.
summary.en: This episode of Dwarkesh Podcast features Ada Palmer discussing Niccolò Machiavelli, highlighting how he is perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time. The conversation covers Machiavelli's career as a high-level diplomat for Florence, his observations of European rulers, and his political downfall when the Medici retook Florence in 1513, leading to his torture and exile. Despite this, Machiavelli dedicated his seminal book, The Prince, to Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici, the leader of the regime responsible for his exile, aiming to use his insights to protect Florence. The episode explores Machiavelli's lessons on power, politics, and human nature and how his experiences shaped his work. Ada Palmer is also launching a new podcast focusing on Machiavelli, making this episode useful for those interested in deeper understanding of his work and historical context.
summary.zh: 本期Dwarkesh播客，Ada Palmer详细讲述尼科洛·马基雅维利，强调他可能是史上最被误解的思想家。讨论包括他作为佛罗伦萨高级外交官的生涯、对欧洲统治者的观察，以及1513年美第奇家族收复佛罗伦萨导致他的政治倒台、酷刑与流放。尽管如此，他仍将其代表作《君主论》献给导致他流放的政权领导人——洛伦佐·迪·皮耶罗·德·美第奇，旨在用洞察力保护佛罗伦萨。节目探讨了马基雅维利对权力、政治和人性的教训，以及他的经历如何塑造其著作。Ada Palmer亦将推出聚焦马基雅维利的新播客，对想深入理解其作品和历史背景者尤具价值。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast Dwarkesh, Ada Palmer analiza en profundidad a Niccolò Machiavelli, destacando cómo es posiblemente el pensador más malinterpretado de todos los tiempos. La conversación cubre su carrera como diplomático de alto nivel para Florencia, sus observaciones sobre los gobernantes europeos y su caída política tras la recuperación de Florencia por los Medici en 1513, que llevó a su tortura y exilio. A pesar de ello, dedicó su obra seminal, El Príncipe, a Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici, líder del régimen responsable de su exilio, con la intención de proteger Florencia. El episodio explora las lecciones de Machiavelli sobre poder, política y naturaleza humana, y cómo sus experiencias moldearon su trabajo. Ada Palmer también lanzará un nuevo podcast centrado en Machiavelli, siendo útil para quienes busquen una comprensión más profunda de su obra y contexto histórico.

title.original: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
title.en: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
title.zh: 莎拉·佩恩：普京和习近平无法逃脱地理环境
title.es: Sarah Paine – Por qué Putin y Xi no pueden escapar a la geografía

summary.original: Military historian Sarah Paine provides a lecture on the geopolitical framework shaping global power over two millennia, focusing on how geography constrains leaders like Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping. The episode explores long-standing geographic influences on political power, offering listeners a historical perspective on current geopolitical dynamics.
summary.en: Military historian Sarah Paine provides a lecture on the geopolitical framework shaping global power over two millennia, focusing on how geography constrains leaders like Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping. The episode explores long-standing geographic influences on political power, offering listeners a historical perspective on current geopolitical dynamics.
summary.zh: 军事历史学家莎拉·佩恩讲述了塑造全球力量的两千年地缘框架，重点说明地理如何限制普京和习近平等领导人，探讨长期影响政治权力的地理因素，为听众提供当前地缘政治动态的历史视角。
summary.es: La historiadora militar Sarah Paine ofrece una charla sobre el marco geopolítico que ha moldeado el poder global por dos milenios, enfocándose en cómo la geografía limita a líderes como Vladimir Putin y Xi Jinping, explorando influencias geográficas históricas en el poder político y ofreciendo perspectivas históricas sobre la dinámica geopolítica actual.

title.original: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (FarmVille, Words with Friends, & more)
title.en: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (FarmVille, Words with Friends, & more)
title.zh: 成功产品背后的隐秘模式 | 马克·平卡斯（FarmVille，Words with Friends等）
title.es: El patrón oculto detrás de productos exitosos | Mark Pincus (FarmVille, Words with Friends, y más)

summary.original: This episode of Lenny's Podcast features Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, the company known for consumer products like Words With Friends, FarmVille, and Zynga Poker. The conversation delves into the hidden patterns behind creating successful products, drawing from Pincus's extensive experience in launching multiple hit consumer apps.
summary.en: This episode of Lenny's Podcast features Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, the company known for consumer products like Words With Friends, FarmVille, and Zynga Poker. The conversation delves into the hidden patterns behind creating successful products, drawing from Pincus's extensive experience in launching multiple hit consumer apps.
summary.zh: 本期节目邀请Zynga创始人马克·平卡斯谈论成功产品的隐秘模式，结合他在推出多款热门消费者应用（如Words With Friends、FarmVille和Zynga Poker）的丰富经验，深入探讨产品成功的关键因素。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast de Lenny presenta a Mark Pincus, fundador de Zynga, conocido por productos como Words With Friends, FarmVille y Zynga Poker, quien profundiza en los patrones ocultos detrás de productos exitosos basándose en su amplia experiencia lanzando múltiples aplicaciones populares de consumo.

title.original: Tony Fadell: How to build real taste (and why AI makes it matter more)
title.en: Tony Fadell: How to build real taste (and why AI makes it matter more)
title.zh: Tony Fadell：如何打造真正的品味（以及为什么AI让这更重要）
title.es: Tony Fadell: Cómo construir un verdadero gusto (y por qué la IA lo hace más importante)

summary.original: Tony Fadell, who created the iPod, co-created the iPhone, and founded Nest (sold to Google), appears on Lenny's Podcast to discuss how to build authentic taste in product design. The episode also explores why AI now makes cultivating genuine taste increasingly important, reflecting on Fadell's history of innovation and his co-authored patents.
summary.en: Tony Fadell, who created the iPod, co-created the iPhone, and founded Nest (sold to Google), appears on Lenny's Podcast to discuss how to build authentic taste in product design. The episode also explores why AI now makes cultivating genuine taste increasingly important, reflecting on Fadell's history of innovation and his co-authored patents.
summary.zh: Tony Fadell，iPod创造者、iPhone联合创始人及Nest创始人（后售予谷歌），在Lenny播客中讨论如何在产品设计中打造真实的品味，以及AI为何让培养真品味更为重要，回顾其创新历程和共创专利。
summary.es: Tony Fadell, creador del iPod, co-creador del iPhone y fundador de Nest (vendida a Google), participa en el podcast de Lenny para discutir cómo construir un gusto auténtico en el diseño de productos y por qué la IA hace que cultivar un gusto genuino sea cada vez más importante, reflexionando sobre su historial de innovación y patentes coautoradas.

title.original: Anthropic Expert: Rethink What's Possible With Fable 5
title.en: Anthropic Expert: Rethink What's Possible With Fable 5
title.zh: Anthropic专家：重新思考Fable 5的可能性
title.es: Experto de Anthropic: Repensar lo posible con Fable 5

summary.original: This AI & I episode includes insights from an Anthropic expert about Fable 5, inviting listeners to rethink what is possible with this AI technology. The description also mentions Mike Krieger, cofounder of Instagram, who is active at the frontier of AI-native development, suggesting thoughtful discussion on AI's evolving roles.
summary.en: This AI & I episode includes insights from an Anthropic expert about Fable 5, inviting listeners to rethink what is possible with this AI technology. The description also mentions Mike Krieger, cofounder of Instagram, who is active at the frontier of AI-native development, suggesting thoughtful discussion on AI's evolving roles.
summary.zh: 本期《AI & I》节目邀请Anthropic专家分享关于Fable 5的见解，鼓励听众重新思考这项AI技术的潜力。介绍中还提及Instagram联合创始人Mike Krieger，他活跃于AI原生开发前沿，启发对AI演变角色的深入讨论。
summary.es: Este episodio de AI & I incluye perspectivas de un experto de Anthropic sobre Fable 5, invitando a repensar lo que es posible con esta tecnología de IA. La descripción también menciona a Mike Krieger, cofundador de Instagram, activo en el desarrollo nativo de IA, sugiriendo una discusión reflexiva sobre los roles evolutivos de la IA.

title.original: The SaaS Apocalypse Is a Goldmine With Figma’s Matt Colyer
title.en: The SaaS Apocalypse Is a Goldmine With Figma’s Matt Colyer
title.zh: SaaS末日是金矿：与Figma的Matt Colyer对话
title.es: El Apocalipsis del SaaS es una Mina de Oro con Matt Colyer de Figma

summary.original: Figma’s director of product management, Matt Colyer, talks on AI & I about the so-called "SaaSpocalypse," the concern that AI will obsolete software-as-a-service. The episode hints at Colyer's perspective that this shift is a valuable opportunity rather than a threat, which could be informative for those interested in AI's impact on SaaS products.
summary.en: Figma’s director of product management, Matt Colyer, talks on AI & I about the so-called "SaaSpocalypse," the concern that AI will obsolete software-as-a-service. The episode hints at Colyer's perspective that this shift is a valuable opportunity rather than a threat, which could be informative for those interested in AI's impact on SaaS products.
summary.zh: Figma产品管理总监Matt Colyer在《AI & I》中谈及所谓的“SaaSpocalypse”，即AI可能使SaaS过时的担忧。本集透露他视这场变革为宝贵机遇而非威胁，适合关注AI对SaaS产品影响的听众。
summary.es: Matt Colyer, director de gestión de productos de Figma, habla en AI & I sobre el llamado "SaaSpocalypse", la preocupación de que la IA hará obsoletos los servicios de software (SaaS). El episodio sugiere que Colyer ve este cambio como una oportunidad valiosa más que una amenaza, información útil para quienes siguen el impacto de la IA en productos SaaS.

title.original: NVIDIA's Jensen Huang on Building the Dynamo of the Intelligence Age
title.en: NVIDIA's Jensen Huang on Building the Dynamo of the Intelligence Age
title.zh: NVIDIA的黄仁勋谈打造智能时代的发电机
title.es: Jensen Huang de NVIDIA sobre Construir el Dynamo de la Era de la Inteligencia

summary.original: This episode features Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA, discussing a major shift in computing that he describes as the biggest in 60 years—a transition from data retrieval to new computing paradigms. The episode explores NVIDIA's role in this transformation and offers insights about building what Huang calls the "Dynamo of the Intelligence Age." Listeners interested in AI, technology innovation, and the future of computing may find this podcast especially insightful given Huang's perspective on ongoing changes in data center computing.
summary.en: This episode features Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA, discussing a major shift in computing that he describes as the biggest in 60 years—a transition from data retrieval to new computing paradigms. The episode explores NVIDIA's role in this transformation and offers insights about building what Huang calls the "Dynamo of the Intelligence Age." Listeners interested in AI, technology innovation, and the future of computing may find this podcast especially insightful given Huang's perspective on ongoing changes in data center computing.
summary.zh: 本期节目邀请NVIDIA创始人兼CEO黄仁勋，讨论他称之为60年来最大计算变革——从数据检索到新计算范式的转变。节目探讨NVIDIA在此次变革中的角色，并分享打造“智能时代发电机”的见解，适合关注AI、技术创新和计算未来的听众。
summary.es: Este episodio presenta a Jensen Huang, fundador y CEO de NVIDIA, hablando sobre un cambio mayor en la computación que él considera el más grande en 60 años, una transición del acceso a datos a nuevos paradigmas computacionales. Explora el papel de NVIDIA en esta transformación y ofrece perspectivas sobre construir el "Dynamo de la Era de la Inteligencia", ideal para interesados en IA, innovación tecnológica y futuro de la computación.

title.original: Simulating Humans at Scale: Simile's Joon Sung Park
title.en: Simulating Humans at Scale: Simile's Joon Sung Park
title.zh: 大规模模拟人类：Simile的Joon Sung Park
title.es: Simulando Humanos a Gran Escala: Joon Sung Park de Simile

summary.original: Joon Sung Park of Simile appears in this podcast episode to talk about challenges in developing superintelligent models that better simulate human behavior. The content focuses on the difference between improving objective problem-solving abilities of AI and enabling AI to realistically mimic human social behaviors at scale. This episode could appeal to listeners curious about AI advancements specifically related to human simulation and behavior modeling, according to the episode title and description.
summary.en: Joon Sung Park of Simile appears in this podcast episode to talk about challenges in developing superintelligent models that better simulate human behavior. The content focuses on the difference between improving objective problem-solving abilities of AI and enabling AI to realistically mimic human social behaviors at scale. This episode could appeal to listeners curious about AI advancements specifically related to human simulation and behavior modeling, according to the episode title and description.
summary.zh: Simile的Joon Sung Park在本集播客中探讨打造超智能模型以更好模拟人类行为的挑战。内容聚焦提升AI客观解决问题能力与使其真实模仿人类社交行为的区别，适合对人类模拟和行为建模相关AI进展感兴趣的听众。
summary.es: Joon Sung Park de Simile aparece en este episodio para hablar sobre los retos en desarrollar modelos superinteligentes que simulen mejor el comportamiento humano. El contenido se centra en la diferencia entre mejorar las habilidades objetivas de resolución de problemas de la IA y permitir que la IA imite realísticamente los comportamientos sociales humanos a gran escala. Ideal para quienes interesen en avances de IA relacionados con simulación humana y modelado de comportamiento.

title.original: Biggest Mysteries in Physics: Antimatter, Dark Energy & ToE - Don Lincoln | Lex Fridman Podcast #497
title.en: Biggest Mysteries in Physics: Antimatter, Dark Energy & ToE - Don Lincoln | Lex Fridman Podcast #497
title.zh: 物理学最大谜团：反物质、暗能量与万物理论 - Don Lincoln｜Lex Fridman播客#497
title.es: Los Mayores Misterios de la Física: Antimateria, Energía Oscura y Teoría del Todo - Don Lincoln | Podcast de Lex Fridman #497

summary.original: This podcast episode features Don Lincoln, a particle physicist at Fermilab, discussing major open questions in physics such as antimatter, dark energy, and the theory of everything (ToE). Given Lincoln's experience in high energy physics, the conversation likely delves into frontiers of fundamental physics research. Those interested in physics mysteries and scientific exploration might find this episode valuable, as suggested by the episode title and description.
summary.en: This podcast episode features Don Lincoln, a particle physicist at Fermilab, discussing major open questions in physics such as antimatter, dark energy, and the theory of everything (ToE). Given Lincoln's experience in high energy physics, the conversation likely delves into frontiers of fundamental physics research. Those interested in physics mysteries and scientific exploration might find this episode valuable, as suggested by the episode title and description.
summary.zh: 本期播客邀请Fermilab粒子物理学家Don Lincoln，讨论物理学中的重大未解问题，如反物质、暗能量及万物理论。Lincoln在高能物理领域的经验使对话深入基础物理前沿，适合对物理谜团和科学探索感兴趣的听众。
summary.es: Este episodio presenta a Don Lincoln, físico de partículas en Fermilab, discutiendo grandes interrogantes abiertos en física como la antimateria, energía oscura y la teoría del todo (ToE). Dada su experiencia en física de altas energías, la conversación profundiza en fronteras de la investigación física fundamental, ideal para interesados en misterios físicos y exploración científica.

title.original: “Curing All Disease by next century is too conservative" - Mark Zuckerberg
title.en: “Curing All Disease by next century is too conservative" - Mark Zuckerberg
title.zh: “到下个世纪治愈所有疾病仍显保守" - 马克·扎克伯格
title.es: “Curar todas las enfermedades para el próximo siglo es demasiado conservador" - Mark Zuckerberg

summary.original: The episode features a discussion about Biohub's ambitious goal to cure, prevent, and manage all diseases by the end of the century. Mark Zuckerberg participates in this conversation, which reflects on the convergence of scientific advances and the initial goals set a decade ago. According to the description, the title implies that aiming to cure all disease by the next century might be a conservative estimate. This episode may be of interest to those following biomedical innovation and long-term visions in health research.
summary.en: The episode features a discussion about Biohub's ambitious goal to cure, prevent, and manage all diseases by the end of the century. Mark Zuckerberg participates in this conversation, which reflects on the convergence of scientific advances and the initial goals set a decade ago. According to the description, the title implies that aiming to cure all disease by the next century might be a conservative estimate. This episode may be of interest to those following biomedical innovation and long-term visions in health research.
summary.zh: 本集探讨了Biohub的雄心目标，即在本世纪末治愈、预防和管理所有疾病。马克·扎克伯格参与了此讨论，回顾科学进展与十年前设立的初衷。标题暗示治愈所有疾病的目标可能保守。此集适合关注生物医学创新与健康研究长远愿景者。
summary.es: El episodio analiza el ambicioso objetivo de Biohub de curar, prevenir y manejar todas las enfermedades para finales de este siglo. Mark Zuckerberg participa en la conversación, reflexionando sobre la convergencia de avances científicos y las metas iniciales establecidas hace una década. El título sugiere que aspirar a curar todas las enfermedades para el próximo siglo podría ser una estimación conservadora. Este episodio interesa a quienes siguen la innovación biomédica y visiones a largo plazo en investigación sanitaria.

title.original: We Need An Ecosystem in AI, And Every Company Can Win A Place In It
title.en: We Need An Ecosystem in AI, And Every Company Can Win A Place In It
title.zh: 我们需要一个AI生态系统，每家公司都能赢得一席之地
title.es: Necesitamos un ecosistema en IA, y cada empresa puede ganar un lugar en él

summary.original: This episode from No Priors on YouTube discusses the need for an ecosystem in AI, suggesting that every company can find a place within it. The title and meta description indicate a focus on what it means for businesses to truly operate at the AI frontier, featuring a special crossover episode recorded at Microsoft Build with Sarah Guo and Elad Gil. Listeners interested in AI and tech business strategies may find value in this conversation, though specific details about the guests or in-depth topics were not provided in the evidence.
summary.en: This episode from No Priors on YouTube discusses the need for an ecosystem in AI, suggesting that every company can find a place within it. The title and meta description indicate a focus on what it means for businesses to truly operate at the AI frontier, featuring a special crossover episode recorded at Microsoft Build with Sarah Guo and Elad Gil. Listeners interested in AI and tech business strategies may find value in this conversation, though specific details about the guests or in-depth topics were not provided in the evidence.
summary.zh: 本集出自YouTube的No Priors，讨论AI生态系统的必要性，强调每家公司都可在其中找到位置。内容聚焦企业如何在AI前沿真正运营，并收录微软Build大会录制的特别跨界对话，嘉宾包括Sarah Guo和Elad Gil。对关注AI与科技商业策略者有价值。
summary.es: Este episodio de No Priors en YouTube discute la necesidad de un ecosistema en IA, sugiriendo que cada empresa puede encontrar su lugar dentro de él. El título y la descripción señalan un enfoque en lo que significa para las empresas operar en la vanguardia de la IA, con un episodio especial grabado en Microsoft Build con Sarah Guo y Elad Gil. Los interesados en IA y estrategias comerciales tecnológicas encontrarán valor en esta conversación.

title.original: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (founder of Zynga)
title.en: The hidden pattern behind successful products | Mark Pincus (founder of Zynga)
title.zh: 成功产品背后的隐藏模式
title.es: El patrón oculto detrás de los productos exitosos

summary.original: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares lessons from creating numerous successful consumer games including Words With Friends and FarmVille. With eight out of ten major launches becoming hits, Pincus discusses frameworks such as “Proven, Better, New” for product success and why less ambitious ideas can lead to greater innovation. The conversation includes his views on intuition, idea validation, and raising children in the AI era. Pincus also announces his forthcoming book, Life at the Speed of Play. This episode offers valuable insights for product builders and startup enthusiasts.
summary.en: In this episode of Lenny's Podcast, Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares lessons from creating numerous successful consumer games including Words With Friends and FarmVille. With eight out of ten major launches becoming hits, Pincus discusses frameworks such as “Proven, Better, New” for product success and why less ambitious ideas can lead to greater innovation. The conversation includes his views on intuition, idea validation, and raising children in the AI era. Pincus also announces his forthcoming book, Life at the Speed of Play. This episode offers valuable insights for product builders and startup enthusiasts.
summary.zh: 本期Lenny播客邀请Zynga创始人Mark Pincus分享打造热门游戏的经验，探讨“经过验证、更好、新颖”的产品成功框架，并讨论直觉、验证创意及AI时代育儿观点。Pincus还介绍即将出版的新书《Life at the Speed of Play》。
summary.es: En este episodio del Podcast de Lenny, Mark Pincus, fundador de Zynga, comparte lecciones sobre juegos exitosos como Words With Friends y FarmVille, discute el marco “Probado, Mejor, Nuevo” para el éxito de productos y sus puntos de vista sobre intuición, validación de ideas y crianza en la era de la IA. Anuncia su próximo libro Life at the Speed of Play.

title.original: Biohub: The Future of Biology is Open-Source with Co-Founders Mark Zuckerberg, Priscilla Chan, and Head of Science Alex Rives
title.en: Biohub: The Future of Biology is Open-Source with Co-Founders Mark Zuckerberg, Priscilla Chan, and Head of Science Alex Rives
title.zh: Biohub：共同创始人马克·扎克伯格、普莉希拉·陈和科学主管亚历克斯·里维斯谈开放源代码的生物学未来
title.es: Biohub: El futuro de la biología es de código abierto con los cofundadores Mark Zuckerberg, Priscilla Chan y el jefe de ciencia Alex Rives

summary.original: This No Priors podcast episode features Biohub co-founders Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan along with Biohub Head of Science Alex Rives. They discuss Biohub's origins and its goal of curing, preventing, and managing all disease by the end of the century. The episode addresses how the convergence of frontier AI and biological data has influenced their progress and outlook. Listeners interested in open-source biology and medical innovation may find this episode informative.
summary.en: This No Priors podcast episode features Biohub co-founders Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan along with Biohub Head of Science Alex Rives. They discuss Biohub's origins and its goal of curing, preventing, and managing all disease by the end of the century. The episode addresses how the convergence of frontier AI and biological data has influenced their progress and outlook. Listeners interested in open-source biology and medical innovation may find this episode informative.
summary.zh: 这期No Priors播客邀请Biohub联合创始人马克·扎克伯格、普莉希拉·陈及科学负责人亚历克斯·里维斯，讨论Biohub的起源及其在本世纪末治愈、预防和管理所有疾病的目标。探讨前沿AI与生物数据融合如何影响他们的进展和展望，适合关注开放源代码生物学和医疗创新的听众。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast No Priors presenta a los cofundadores de Biohub Mark Zuckerberg y Priscilla Chan junto con el jefe de ciencia Alex Rives. Discuten los orígenes de Biohub y su meta de curar, prevenir y manejar todas las enfermedades para finales de siglo. Abordan cómo la convergencia de IA avanzada y datos biológicos ha influido en su progreso y perspectiva. Interesante para quienes siguen la biología de código abierto y la innovación médica.

title.original: Father of the iPod and iPhone on building taste, judgment, and creativity in the AI era | Tony Fadell
title.en: Father of the iPod and iPhone on building taste, judgment, and creativity in the AI era | Tony Fadell
title.zh: iPod和iPhone之父谈AI时代的品味、判断力与创造力 | 托尼·法德尔
title.es: El padre del iPod y iPhone sobre construir gusto, juicio y creatividad en la era de la IA | Tony Fadell

summary.original: This episode of Lenny's Podcast features Tony Fadell, who created the iPod, co-created the iPhone, and founded Nest. The in-depth interview covers topics such as the debates over the iPhone's physical keyboard, the role of opinion-based decisions in early product versions, the importance of marketing alongside product design, and the future role of voice interfaces with AI. The episode offers valuable insights into creativity, taste, and judgment in the AI era, making it compelling for listeners interested in product design and innovation.
summary.en: This episode of Lenny's Podcast features Tony Fadell, who created the iPod, co-created the iPhone, and founded Nest. The in-depth interview covers topics such as the debates over the iPhone's physical keyboard, the role of opinion-based decisions in early product versions, the importance of marketing alongside product design, and the future role of voice interfaces with AI. The episode offers valuable insights into creativity, taste, and judgment in the AI era, making it compelling for listeners interested in product design and innovation.
summary.zh: Lenny播客本期邀请托尼·法德尔——iPod创造者、iPhone联合创造者及Nest创始人。访谈深入探讨早期iPhone物理键盘争议、基于观点决策的重要性、市场营销与产品设计的结合，以及未来AI语音界面的角色。对关注产品设计与创新、AI时代创造力的听众极具价值。
summary.es: Este episodio del podcast Lenny presenta a Tony Fadell, creador del iPod, co-creador del iPhone y fundador de Nest. La entrevista aborda debates sobre el teclado físico del iPhone, el papel de decisiones basadas en opinión en versiones tempranas, la importancia del marketing junto al diseño de producto, y el papel futuro de interfaces de voz con IA. Ofrece valiosas ideas sobre creatividad, gusto y juicio en la era de la IA, interesante para quienes siguen diseño e innovación de productos.

title.original: Alex Imas and Phil Trammell – What remains scarce after AGI?
title.en: Alex Imas and Phil Trammell – What remains scarce after AGI?
title.zh: Alex Imas 和 Phil Trammell：AGI之后什么依然稀缺？
title.es: Alex Imas y Phil Trammell – ¿Qué sigue siendo escaso después de la AGI?

summary.original: This episode of the Dwarkesh Podcast features Alex Imas and Phil Trammell discussing the economics of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). They explore important questions such as optimal taxation and redistribution of AI-generated wealth, integration of countries outside the AI supply chain, and the future of inequality. The discussion includes ideas on scarcity in the human sector and the challenges of a machine-driven economy, with detailed conversation points provided in the transcript and timestamps. This episode offers valuable economic insights relevant to the societal impacts of advanced AI.
summary.en: This episode of the Dwarkesh Podcast features Alex Imas and Phil Trammell discussing the economics of Artificial General Intelligence (AGI). They explore important questions such as optimal taxation and redistribution of AI-generated wealth, integration of countries outside the AI supply chain, and the future of inequality. The discussion includes ideas on scarcity in the human sector and the challenges of a machine-driven economy, with detailed conversation points provided in the transcript and timestamps. This episode offers valuable economic insights relevant to the societal impacts of advanced AI.
summary.zh: Dwarkesh播客本集中，Alex Imas和Phil Trammell探讨人工通用智能（AGI）的经济学，包括AI财富的最佳税收与再分配、非AI供应链国家的整合以及未来不平等问题。讨论涉及人类领域的稀缺性及机器驱动经济的挑战，内容详见抄本与时间戳。该集提供关于高级AI社会影响的宝贵经济见解。
summary.es: En este episodio del podcast Dwarkesh, Alex Imas y Phil Trammell discuten la economía de la Inteligencia Artificial General (AGI). Exploran temas como la tributación óptima y la redistribución de la riqueza generada por IA, la integración de países fuera de la cadena de suministro de IA y el futuro de la desigualdad. También abordan la escasez en el sector humano y los retos de una economía impulsada por máquinas, con detalles en la transcripción y marcas de tiempo. Este episodio ofrece valiosas ideas económicas relevantes para el impacto social de la IA avanzada.

title.original: We Need An Ecosystem in AI, And Every Company Can Win A Place In It
title.en: We Need An Ecosystem in AI, And Every Company Can Win A Place In It
title.zh: 我们需要一个AI生态系统，每家公司都能占有一席之地
title.es: Necesitamos un ecosistema en IA, y cada compañía puede ganar un lugar en él

summary.original: In this special crossover episode of the No Priors Podcast recorded at Microsoft Build, Sarah Guo, Elad Gil, and host "swyx" speak with Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella. They discuss what it means for businesses to operate at the AI frontier, reflecting on recent Microsoft Build breakthroughs. The conversation touches on the future of AI platforms, software development, and the tech ecosystem, highlighting how every company may find a place in this evolving AI ecosystem. This episode offers insights into strategic shifts in AI adoption and is valuable for listeners interested in the intersection of AI innovation and business strategy.
summary.en: In this special crossover episode of the No Priors Podcast recorded at Microsoft Build, Sarah Guo, Elad Gil, and host "swyx" speak with Microsoft Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella. They discuss what it means for businesses to operate at the AI frontier, reflecting on recent Microsoft Build breakthroughs. The conversation touches on the future of AI platforms, software development, and the tech ecosystem, highlighting how every company may find a place in this evolving AI ecosystem. This episode offers insights into strategic shifts in AI adoption and is valuable for listeners interested in the intersection of AI innovation and business strategy.
summary.zh: 这期No Priors播客特别跨界集在Microsoft Build录制，Sarah Guo、Elad Gil与主持人“swyx”以及微软董事长兼CEO萨提亚·纳德拉对话。讨论了企业如何在AI前沿运营，回顾近期Microsoft Build的突破，触及AI平台、软件开发和技术生态未来，强调每家公司均有机会在AI生态中找到位置。适合关注AI创新与商业策略交集的听众。
summary.es: En este episodio especial crossover del podcast No Priors grabado en Microsoft Build, Sarah Guo, Elad Gil y el anfitrión “swyx” conversan con Satya Nadella, presidente y CEO de Microsoft. Discuten qué significa para las empresas operar en la frontera de la IA, reflexionando sobre los avances recientes en Microsoft Build. La conversación aborda el futuro de plataformas de IA, desarrollo de software y el ecosistema tecnológico, destacando cómo cada empresa puede encontrar un lugar en este ecosistema de IA en evolución. Ofrece perspectivas sobre cambios estratégicos en la adopción de IA, valioso para quienes siguen la innovación en IA y estrategia empresarial.

title.original: #497 – Biggest Mysteries in Physics: Antimatter, Dark Energy & ToE – Don Lincoln
title.en: #497 – Biggest Mysteries in Physics: Antimatter, Dark Energy & ToE – Don Lincoln
title.zh: #497 – 物理学中的最大谜题：反物质、暗能量与万物理论（ToE）——Don Lincoln
title.es: #497 – Los mayores misterios en física: Antimateria, energía oscura y la Teoría del Todo (ToE) – Don Lincoln

summary.original: Episode #497 of the Lex Fridman Podcast features Don Lincoln, a particle physicist at Fermilab with decades of experience in high energy physics. The discussion covers major mysteries in physics such as antimatter, dark energy, and the pursuit of a Theory of Everything (ToE). The episode explores topics like Einstein's relativity theories, electroweak force, particle colliders, the Higgs boson discovery, and the physics of empty space. This conversation provides a deep dive into fundamental physics questions, making it a compelling listen for those fascinated by cutting-edge scientific understanding of the universe.
summary.en: Episode #497 of the Lex Fridman Podcast features Don Lincoln, a particle physicist at Fermilab with decades of experience in high energy physics. The discussion covers major mysteries in physics such as antimatter, dark energy, and the pursuit of a Theory of Everything (ToE). The episode explores topics like Einstein's relativity theories, electroweak force, particle colliders, the Higgs boson discovery, and the physics of empty space. This conversation provides a deep dive into fundamental physics questions, making it a compelling listen for those fascinated by cutting-edge scientific understanding of the universe.
summary.zh: Lex Fridman播客第497期邀请了Fermilab的粒子物理学家Don Lincoln，讨论物理学中的重大谜题，如反物质、暗能量和追求万物理论（ToE）。内容涵盖爱因斯坦的相对论、电弱力、粒子对撞机、希格斯玻色子发现及空洞空间物理，为热衷前沿宇宙科学理解的听众提供深度内容。
summary.es: El episodio #497 del podcast de Lex Fridman presenta a Don Lincoln, físico de partículas en Fermilab con amplia experiencia en física de altas energías. La conversación aborda grandes misterios de la física como la antimateria, la energía oscura y la búsqueda de una Teoría del Todo (ToE). Se exploran teorías de la relatividad de Einstein, la fuerza electrodébil, colisionadores de partículas, el descubrimiento del bosón de Higgs y la física del espacio vacío, ofreciendo una inmersión profunda para quienes se fascinan con la comprensión científica avanzada del universo.

title.original: #496 – FFmpeg: The Incredible Technology Behind Video on the Internet
title.en: #496 – FFmpeg: The Incredible Technology Behind Video on the Internet
title.zh: #496 – FFmpeg：互联网视频背后的惊人技术
title.es: #496 – FFmpeg: La increíble tecnología detrás del video en internet

summary.original: Episode #496 of the Lex Fridman Podcast features Jean-Baptiste Kempf, lead developer of VLC and president of VideoLAN, alongside Kieran Kunhya, a long-time FFmpeg contributor and codec engineer. They discuss the technology behind FFmpeg, an important tool powering video on the internet, including topics like video codecs, containers, video compression, and the history and development challenges around FFmpeg and VLC. This episode provides an in-depth look at foundational video technology, making it especially worthwhile for listeners interested in programming, multimedia technology, and open-source software development.
summary.en: Episode #496 of the Lex Fridman Podcast features Jean-Baptiste Kempf, lead developer of VLC and president of VideoLAN, alongside Kieran Kunhya, a long-time FFmpeg contributor and codec engineer. They discuss the technology behind FFmpeg, an important tool powering video on the internet, including topics like video codecs, containers, video compression, and the history and development challenges around FFmpeg and VLC. This episode provides an in-depth look at foundational video technology, making it especially worthwhile for listeners interested in programming, multimedia technology, and open-source software development.
summary.zh: Lex Fridman播客第496期邀请VLC主要开发者及VideoLAN主席Jean-Baptiste Kempf，及FFmpeg长期贡献者与编码器工程师Kieran Kunhya，深入讨论FFmpeg背后的技术，包括视频编码器、容器、视频压缩，以及FFmpeg和VLC的发展历史与挑战，为对编程、多媒体技术和开源软件开发感兴趣的听众提供丰富内容。
summary.es: El episodio #496 del podcast de Lex Fridman presenta a Jean-Baptiste Kempf, desarrollador principal de VLC y presidente de VideoLAN, y a Kieran Kunhya, contribuyente de largo tiempo de FFmpeg e ingeniero de códecs. Discuten la tecnología detrás de FFmpeg, herramienta fundamental para el video en internet, abarcando códecs de video, contenedores, compresión y la historia y desafíos en el desarrollo de FFmpeg y VLC. El episodio ofrece una mirada profunda a la tecnología base del video, ideal para interesados en programación, tecnología multimedia y desarrollo de software de código abierto.
